transactions.apps module

class transactions.apps.TransactionsConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]

Bases: AppConfig

name = 'transactions'

transactions.models module

class transactions.models.Budget(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represents a spending limit for a specific category.

Tracks: - Budget amount - Spent amount - Remaining amount - Percentage usage - Start and end dates - Status (active, expired, completed)

Linked to a wallet and category.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

amount

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

category

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

category_id
end_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_end_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateField: end_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)
get_next_by_start_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateField: start_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)

transactions

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

wallet

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

wallet_id
class transactions.models.Category(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represents transaction categories for a specific wallet.

Each category belongs to one wallet. Category names must be unique per wallet. Used for budgeting and expense tracking.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

budgets

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

class transactions.models.SavingGoals(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represents a financial saving goal.

Tracks: - Target amount - Current saved amount - Deadline - Progress percentage - Status (in_progress, complete, failed)

Linked to a wallet.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

choices = [('in_progress', 'In_progress'), ('complete', 'Complete'), ('failed', 'Failed')]

class transactions.models.Transaction(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represents all financial transactions in the system.

Supports types: - income - expense - saving - send

Can be linked to: - Wallet - Budget - SavingGoals - Receiver Wallet (for transfers)

Automatically records creation date.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

TYPE_CHOICES = [('income', 'Income'), ('expense', 'Expense'), ('saving', 'Saving'), ('send', 'Sending')]

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

transactions.tests module

transactions.url module

transactions.views module

transactions.views.delete_budget(request, id)[source]

-delete budget

transactions.views.saving_delete(request, id)[source]

-delete exists savings

transactions.views.view_add_transaction(request)[source]

Adds expense or saving transaction.

  • Updates budget or saving goal

  • Updates wallet balance

  • Triggers alerts if necessary

transactions.views.view_budget(request)[source]

Manages budgets.

  • Create budget

  • Update budget

  • Calculate percentage

  • Track spending

transactions.views.view_categories(request)[source]

Manage categories -create or delete categories

transactions.views.view_saving_goals(request)[source]

Manages saving goals.

  • Create goal

  • Update goal

  • Track progress

transactions.views.view_send(request)[source]

Handles money transfer between users.

  • Validates recipient

  • Checks balance

  • Applies fee logic

  • Updates both wallets

  • Creates transaction record

  • Sends notifications

transactions.views.view_transaction(request)[source]

Displays all transactions for the current user’s wallet.
